scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, verified against Calendly's published OpenAPI 3.1 spec (github.com/api-evangelist/calendly, openapi/calendly-scheduling-api-openapi.yml — 35 method+path endpoints across 30 paths, matching developer.calendly.com/api-docs's documented resource categories). 11 read streams and 8 writes are now covered; every other endpoint is a single-resource duplicate of an already-covered list stream, a GDPR/account-settings action out of scope for a scheduling-data connector, or a narrower compound-write variant deferred to a later wave.

excluded.reason: single-scheduled-event detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the scheduled_events list stream
single-event-type detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the event_types list stream
single-membership detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the organization_memberships list stream
single-webhook detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the webhook_subscriptions list stream
single-schedule detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the user_availability_schedules list stream
single-routing-form detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the routing_forms list stream
single-submission detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the routing_form_submissions list stream
single-invitee detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the invitees fan_out stream

single-user detail fetch by arbitrary uuid; the users stream already covers the authenticated user (GET /users/me), the only user this bundle's api_key credential can name without a separate user-listing endpoint (Calendly publishes none)
computes available booking time slots for an event type over an arbitrary caller-supplied time window at query time; a derived availability calculation, not a persisted, syncable record set

requires an arbitrary caller-supplied start_time/end_time window per call (not a natural full/incremental list), on top of the same user-uri-required constraint as user_availability_schedules; a point-in-time availability check, not a syncable object stream

lists an organization's pending invitations; a narrower, less useful view than organization_memberships (accepted members) for a sync-oriented connector, and not the resource the invite_user_to_organization write acts on reading back
single-invitation detail fetch; same reasoning as the list endpoint above
revokes a pending organization invitation; narrow lifecycle action on a resource this bundle does not stream, deferred to a later wave
GDPR-style bulk deletion of invitee personal data across the account by email address; irreversible compliance action, never a safe automated write
